evals.prompt: I need to start the OpenCode server for my project at /Users/fan/my-app so I can delegate some work to it later
evals.expected_output: Runs ensure-serve via Bash with --directory /Users/fan/my-app. Does NOT spawn an agent subagent.

assertions.text: Uses Bash to call opencode-companion.mjs ensure-serve
Passes --directory /Users/fan/my-app
Does NOT spawn an Agent or route to opencode-agent

evals.prompt: delegate an analysis of the current project to OpenCode — look at the test coverage and suggest improvements
evals.expected_output: Routes through opencode-agent, which ultimately calls opencode-companion.mjs task for the analysis request.

assertions.text: Routes the request through opencode-agent
Delegates the first hop to opencode-agent rather than invoking opencode-companion.mjs task directly from the parent
The agent ultimately calls opencode-companion.mjs task
Prompt forwarded to companion includes the analysis request

evals.prompt: continue the previous OpenCode session ses_abcdef123, the project was /Users/fan/my-app — tell it to fix the remaining test failures in auth.test.ts
evals.expected_output: Routes through opencode-agent and continues the existing OpenCode session with --session ses_abcdef123 --directory /Users/fan/my-app.

assertions.text: Routes the request through opencode-agent
Delegates the first hop to opencode-agent rather than invoking opencode-companion.mjs task directly from the parent
Passes --session ses_abcdef123
Passes --directory /Users/fan/my-app
Starts by forwarding to opencode-agent / opencode-companion.mjs task with --session ses_abcdef123 --directory /Users/fan/my-app; does not do a preliminary liveness check or create a fresh session

evals.prompt: use an agent to handle this OpenCode task: review the API endpoints for security issues
evals.expected_output: Routes through opencode-agent and the agent ultimately calls opencode-companion.mjs task.

assertions.text: Routes the request through the opencode-agent wrapper, regardless of the underlying subagent mechanism
Delegates the first hop to opencode-agent rather than invoking opencode-companion.mjs task directly from the parent
The agent ultimately calls opencode-companion.mjs task

evals.prompt: is OpenCode installed and ready? check if the serve is running
evals.expected_output: Runs check subcommand via Bash directly.

assertions.text: Uses Bash to call opencode-companion.mjs check
Does NOT spawn an Agent subagent

evals.prompt: use the OpenCode agent to run a tiny long-running task: have it sleep for a short time and then report done. Start the initial companion task with an intentionally short timeout so the lifecycle-recovery path is exercised, and do not start a duplicate task if that first wait times out.
evals.expected_output: Routes through opencode-agent, starts one OpenCode task session, and if the initial wait times out it continues the same session lifecycle by attaching with a bounded timeout around 5 minutes, re-checking whether that same session is still active, and looping until the original session finishes or clearly fails.

assertions.text: Routes the request through opencode-agent
Starts the original work with opencode-companion.mjs task
The initial opencode-companion.mjs task call uses a deliberately short --timeout to force lifecycle recovery
If the initial task wait times out, extracts and reuses the same session id instead of starting a duplicate fresh task
Uses opencode-companion.mjs attach with a short bounded timeout around 5 minutes
After an attach timeout or drop, re-checks whether the existing session is still active and continues the loop until completion or a clear failure
Returns the final OpenCode output from the original session verbatim once the lifecycle is complete
